questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3 is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

questions.text: Aluminum, has 3 electrons in its valence shell, therefore it has the ability to form ion:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A (Monovalent) is incorrect because aluminum has 3 valence electrons, which means it can lose 3 electrons to form a stable ion (Al³⁺), not just one.
Option B (Divalent) is incorrect because divalent ions lose or gain 2 electrons, and aluminum does not typically form such ions.
C is correct as 3 electrons in valence means “Trivalent”.
Option D (Tetravalent) is incorrect because tetravalent ions lose or gain 4 electrons, which is not characteristic of aluminum's behavior.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct as any atom, ion or chemical group with 3 electrons in its valence shell is called "trivalent".

questions.text: The bond formed by the complete transfer of electron is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An ionic bond involves the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of ions. This occurs typically between metals and non-metals, where the metal loses electrons to become a cation and the non-metal gains electrons to become an anion. The electrostatic attraction between these oppositely charged ions forms the ionic bond.

A covalent bond is characterized by the sharing of electrons between atoms, rather than a complete transfer. This bond typically forms between non-metal atoms that have similar electronegativities, allowing them to share electrons to achieve a full outer shell.

A metallic bond is found in metals, where electrons are not transferred or shared between specific atoms but are instead delocalized across a lattice of metal ions. This 'sea of electrons' provides metals with unique properties such as conductivity and malleability.

A polar covalent bond is a type of covalent bond where the electrons are shared unequally between the atoms, leading to a dipole moment. This occurs when one atom is more electronegative than the other, causing a partial positive charge on one atom and a partial negative charge on the other.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A) Ionic bond. An ionic bond is formed by the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of oppositely charged ions that are held together by electrostatic forces.

questions.text: The ionization energy of group _shows abnormal trend.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because the elements in groups 3A and 4A generally exhibit a gradual increase in ionization energy across the periods, without any abnormal trends.
Option B is incorrect because the elements in groups 5A and 6A also show a regular increase in ionization energy across the periods, without any abnormal trends.

Option C is incorrect because the elements in group 6A (also known as group 16) and group 4A (also known as group 14) follow the expected pattern of increasing ionization energy across the periodic table without any abnormal trends.

D is correct.Ionisation energy of group III-A and VI-A elements show abnormal behaviour. For example "Be" of group II-A should have ionisation energy less than "B" of group III-A but "Be" has greater ionisation energy than "B". This is due to electronic configuration Boron(B) that is Orbitals of B are partially filled and it is easy to remove electrons whereas Be has a completely filled s-orbital and thus it is more stable than B.Similarly "O" of group VI-A should have greater ionisation energy than "N" of group V-A but "O" has less ionisation energy than "N" as Oxygens electronic configuration is One electron can easily be removed to make p-orbital half filled and ultimately more stable whereas in N p-orbital is already half filled and it is stable and more energy is required to remove electrons from it, hence it has more ionisation energy.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct as ionization energy generally increases down groups "3A and 6A" however, the trend has two anomalies. The first is between Mg and Al because the outer electron of Mg is in the orbital 3s, whereas that of Al is in 3p. The 3p electron has more energy than the 3s electron, so the ionization energy of Al is actually less than that of Mg. This makes sense because the 3p electron requires less energy to be removed from the atom. Remember that if an electron has more energy, it needs less external energy to be removed from the atom because having more energy makes it more unstable.The second anomaly is between P and S. Although both have outer electrons in the 3p orbital, that of P is unpaired (3p3), but that of S is paired (3p4). 3p orbitals can fit up to 6 electrons, but if there are 3 or fewer electrons, they are unpaired and take up a "space" just for themselves. Since the fourth electron in 3p4 is paired, it will experience a repulsive force from the third electron. This makes it easier to remove, and therefore the ionisation energy of S is less than that of P.

questions.text: Electron affinity of an atom is the energy released when an electron is _ to an empty or partially filled orbital of an atom to form _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Removed, Cation: This statement is not correct. Electron affinity is the energy released when an electron is added to an empty or partially filled orbital of an atom, not when it is removed.

b) Added, Cation: This statement is also incorrect. When an electron is added to an atom, it forms an anion, not a cation. An anion is an ion with a negative charge due to the addition of an extra electron.

c) Added, Anion: This statement is correct. Electron affinity refers to the energy released when an electron is added to an atom, resulting in the formation of an anion (negatively charged ion). Anions have a higher electron density than neutral atoms, and the process of adding an electron to an atom is usually exothermic.
d) Removed, Anion: This statement is not correct. Electron affinity is related to the addition of an electron to an atom to form an anion, not the removal of an electron.

questions.explanation: Option CAmount of energy released (or absorbed in some cases) when an electron is added to a gaseous atom to form a negative ion or anion is known as electron affinity.

When an atom loses one or more electrons and becomes positively charged (cation), its electrons are held more tightly due to the increase in nuclear charge. As a result, it requires more energy to remove an electron from a cation.
c) Moderate: This option suggests that the process of removing an electron from a positively charged ion is moderate in difficulty. However, as explained above, it is actually more difficult.
d) None of these: This option implies that none of the provided options are correct. However, option b) "Difficult" is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: b) Difficult: This option correctly states that it is difficult to remove an electron from a positively charged ion compared to a neutral atom. questions.text: Who was able to determine the distance b/w "K+"and "Cl-" in potassium chloride crystal?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Bohr did not determine the distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal. While Bohr made significant contributions to our understanding of atomic structure, he did not work specifically on the crystal structure of potassium chloride. The distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal was determined using X-ray crystallography.

Linus Pauling used X-ray crystallography to determine the atomic structure of various molecules and crystals. In the case of potassium chloride, he directed X-rays at a crystal of the compound, which caused the X-rays to diffract off the atoms in the crystal. By analyzing the diffraction pattern, Pauling was able to determine the positions of the potassium and chloride ions in the crystal lattice, as well as the distance between them. This technique allowed him to make important contributions to our understanding of the structure and properties of various materials.

Jöns Jacob Berzelius was a Swedish chemist who made significant contributions to the development of modern chemical notation and the discovery of several elements. However, he did not work specifically on the crystal structure of potassium chloride or determine the distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal. The distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal was determined using X-ray crystallography, as I mentioned earlier.

Ernest Rutherford was a New Zealand-born physicist who made important contributions to our understanding of atomic structure and radioactivity. However, he did not work specifically on the crystal structure of potassium chloride or determine the distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal. The distance between K+ and Cl- ions in a potassium chloride crystal was determined using X-ray crystallography.

questions.explanation: Linus Pauling used X-ray crystallography to determine the atomic structure of various molecules and crystals. In the case of potassium chloride, he directed X-rays at a crystal of the compound, which caused the X-rays to diffract off the atoms in the crystal. By analyzing the diffraction pattern, Pauling was able to determine the positions of the potassium and chloride ions in the crystal lattice, as well as the distance between them. This technique allowed him to make important contributions to our understanding of the structure and properties of various materials.

questions.text: In H-F bond formation, the fluorine atom completes its _ by acquiring a share of electron from hydrogen:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests that the fluorine atom completes its 2s orbital by acquiring a share of the electron from hydrogen. However, this statement is incorrect. Fluorine has a filled 2s orbital and requires an additional electron to complete its 2p orbital to achieve a stable electron configuration.

Hydrogen, in its neutral state, has one electron in its 1s orbital. Fluorine, on the other hand, has seven electrons in its 2s and 2p orbitals and needs one more electron to achieve a stable, full valence shell. When hydrogen and fluorine come together, hydrogen donates its electron to fluorine, resulting in the formation of a hydride ion (H-) and a fluoride ion (F+). These ions are held together by electrostatic attraction, forming an ionic bond. So, in the context of H-F bond formation, the hydrogen atom donates its electron to fluorine, leading to the completion of fluorine's 2p orbital and the creation of ions rather than the sharing of electrons in a typical covalent bond.

This option is incorrect. The hydrogen atom shares its 1s orbital electron with fluorine to form a covalent bond. However, fluorine does not complete its electron configuration by acquiring this shared electron.
This option is incorrect. Only option B is correct. Fluorine completes its 2p orbital by acquiring a share of the electron from hydrogen.

When hydrogen and fluorine come together, hydrogen donates its electron to fluorine, resulting in the formation of a hydride ion (H-) and a fluoride ion (F+). These ions are held together by electrostatic attraction, forming an ionic bond. So, in the context of H-F bond formation, the hydrogen atom donates its electron to fluorine, leading to the completion of fluorine's 2p orbital and the creation of ions rather than the sharing of electrons in a typical covalent bond.

questions.text: Elements that have high ionization energy value means that they belong to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metals generally have low ionization energies, as they tend to lose electrons easily to form positive ions.
Non-metals can have varying ionization energies, but they generally have lower ionization energies compared to noble gases.
Metalloids can have ionization energies that vary depending on the specific element, but they typically have lower ionization energies compared to noble gases.

Elements that have high ionization energy values belong to the group of noble gases. Noble gases are a group of elements located in Group 18 of the periodic table, including helium (He), neon (Ne), argon (Ar), krypton (Kr), xenon (Xe), and radon (Rn). These elements have a full outer electron shell, making them stable and less likely to lose or gain electrons. As a result, noble gases have high ionization energies, indicating the energy required to remove an electron from an atom is relatively high.

questions.explanation: Elements that have high ionization energy values belong to the group of noble gases. Noble gases are a group of elements located in Group 18 of the periodic table, including helium (He), neon (Ne), argon (Ar), krypton (Kr), xenon (Xe), and radon (Rn). These elements have a full outer electron shell, making them stable and less likely to lose or gain electrons. As a result, noble gases have high ionization energies, indicating the energy required to remove an electron from an atom is relatively high.

questions.explanation: Nyholm and Gillespie (1957) refined the VSEPR model by explaining the important difference between the lone pair and bonding pairs of electrons. While the lone pairs are localised on the central atom, each bonded pair is shared between two atoms. As a result, the lone pair electrons in a molecule occupy more space as compared to the bonding pairs of electrons. This results in greater repulsion between lone pairs of electrons as compared to the lone pair - bond j pair and bond pair - bon pair repulsions. These repulsion effects result in deviations- from idealised shapes and alterations in bond angles in molecules.

options.explanation: To find the ionic character (or the polarity) of a bond, we look at the electronegativity of the two atoms involved. The greater the difference, the more ionic character in the bond. For the entire compound, we can look at its polarity from the arrangement of polar bonds through the 3D structure of the molecule.

Criteria of electronegativity helps us to understand the nature of bond. So, in order to decide the % of ionic nature in a compound, it is better to note the difference of electronegativity between the bonded atoms. If the difference is 1.7 or more than that, then the bond is said to be ionic. Keeping this aspect in view, NaCl has 72% ionic character. CSF has 92% ionic character and calculations tell us that there is no bond with 100% ionic character. (PTB chapter 6: Chemical bonding).

questions.text: When an electron is added, energy is released, so electron affinity is given the?

options.explanation: Positive sign. When an electron is added to an atom, energy is released, so the electron affinity has a negative sign. A positive electron affinity would mean that energy must be added to the atom to add an electron, which is not the case.

A positive electron affinity means that energy must be added to the atom to add an electron, so it is not favorable for the atom to form an anion. A neutral electron affinity means that there is no energy change when an electron is added to the atom, so it is neither favorable nor unfavorable for the atom to form an anion.So, the answer is negative sign.

questions.text: Hydrogen bonding in H − F is stronger than H2O and NH3. The highest boiling point among the three is of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Although HF has strong hydrogen bonding due to the high electronegativity of Fluorine (F), it can only form one hydrogen bond per molecule. Consequently, its boiling point (19.5°C) is lower than that of water, which can form more hydrogen bonds per molecule.
In Ammonia (NH3), the presence of one lone pair on nitrogen limits hydrogen bonding. As a result, NH3 has a boiling point of about -33.3°C, which is lower than that of both HF and H2O.

Water (H2O) can form up to four hydrogen bonds per molecule due to its two hydrogen atoms and two lone pairs on oxygen. This extensive hydrogen bonding results in a high boiling point of 100°C, higher than both HF and NH3.
This option is incorrect as the boiling points of HF, NH3, and H2O are different due to variations in their hydrogen bonding capabilities.

questions.explanation: Each water molecule has two hydrogen atoms. Whereas, each HF molecule has only one hydrogen atom. And hence, water molecule takes part in extensive and more hydrogen bonding than Hydrofluoric acid. Water molecule through their extensive hydrogen bonding forms bulky molecule and it is very difficult to break their bonds. A large amount of energy is required to break all its bonds. Thus H2O has a higher boiling point than HF.

questions.text: Evaporation depends upon:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Evaporation is a surface phenomenon. Increasing the surface area allows more molecules to escape into the air, speeding up evaporation. This is why spreading clothes out to dry makes them dry faster.
Higher temperatures provide more energy to molecules, enabling them to overcome intermolecular forces and escape into the vapor phase more readily, thus increasing the rate of evaporation.

This option is correct. Both surface area and temperature significantly affect evaporation rates. A larger surface area means more molecules can transition to vapor, and higher temperatures provide the energy needed for molecules to escape.
This option is incorrect because both surface area and temperature are known factors that influence the rate of evaporation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that evaporation depends on both surface area and temperature. Evaporation is a surface phenomenon, meaning that more molecules can escape to the air when the surface area is larger. Additionally, increasing the temperature provides more energy to the molecules, allowing them to overcome intermolecular forces more easily and escape into the vapor phase. Therefore, both factors are crucial in determining the rate of evaporation. Options that consider only one factor or neither are incomplete explanations of the process.

questions.text: Which of the following elements has lower first ionization energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Nitrogen has high ionization energy than oxygen due to half filled atomic orbital
Oxygen has low ionization energy than nitrogen because nitrogen has half filled atomic orbital which makes it difficult to remove electron from nitrogen.
Carbon follows normal ionization energy trend

The first ionization of Beryllium is greater than that of Boron because Beryllium has a stable complete electronic configuration (1s2,2s2) so it, require more energy to remove the first electron from it, where as Boron has electronic configuration (1s2,2s2,3s1) which need lesser energy than that of Beryllium.

questions.explanation: The first ionization decreases from beryllium to boron, and from magnesium to aluminum, as electrons from the p-block start to come into play. In the case of boron, which has an electron configuration of 1s2 2s2 2p1, the 2s electrons shield the higher-energy 2p electron from the nucleus, making it slightly easier to remove. A similar effect occurs in aluminum, which has an electron configuration of 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p1. Even though oxygen is to the right of nitrogen in period 2, its first ionization energy is slightly lower than that of nitrogen. Nitrogen has an electron configuration of 1s2 2s2 2p3, which puts one electron in each p orbital, making it a half-filled set of orbitals.Half-filled sets of p orbitals are slightly more stable than those with 2 or 4 electrons, which makes it slightly harder to ionize a nitrogen atom. Oxygen has an electron configuration of 1s2 2s2 2p4, which puts another electron in one p orbital; since this is one electron away from being half-filled, it is slightly easier to remove this additional electron.

questions.text: Considering the molecular orbital theory (MOT), chooses the correct relative energies order.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: σ1s<σ*1s< σ2s< σ* 2s< σ2px< π2py = π2pz

b) σ1s < σ1s < σ2s < σ2s < π2py = π2pz < π2pxThis option is not correct because it does not follow the correct order of molecular orbitals. It places π2py and π2pz orbitals before π2px, which is incorrect.
c) σ1s < σ1s < σ2s < σ2s < π2px = π2pz < π2pyThis option is also not correct because it places π2px and π2pz orbitals before π2py, which is not the correct order.
d) σ1s < σ1s < σ2s < σ2s < π2py < π2pz < π2pxThis option is not correct because it places π2py and π2pz orbitals before π2px, which is incorrect.

questions.text: By raising the temperature 1°C, conductance increases by 2 to 2.5%. Which is due to reduced _ of ions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydration is defined as the process in which a substance or compound reacts with water. It is found to be inversely proportional to conductance such that the ions in hydrated form are found to be surrounded by water molecules which leads to decrease in their conductivity.
Neutralization is defined as the reacting of an acid with a base to produce a salt. The conductivity of the acid-base mixture is found to be dependent on the salt produced,mainly on its solubility.
Hydrolysis is defined as the breakdown of complex or larger molecules into smaller molecules using water.

Ionization is the formation of ions by loss or gain of electrons or the process in which ionic compounds when dissolved in water or fused state,split up into charged particles i.e. ions. Ionization effects condensation in such a way that as the ions increase so does the conductivity.

questions.explanation: Hydration is inversely propotional to conductance. In hydration, ions are surrounded by water molecules so their conductance decreases. So, in order to increase conductance, hydration must decrease to allow electron flow (to conduct electricity).

questions.text: s-sp3 overlap occurs in _ molecule.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cl2 is a diatomic molecule, meaning it consists of two chlorine atoms covalently bonded. Each chlorine atom has 7 valence electrons and needs one more electron to achieve a stable octet. To achieve this, they share one electron each, forming a single covalent bond. This bond is formed by s-sp3 overlap, where the s orbital of one chlorine atom overlaps with one of the three p orbitals of the other chlorine atom.
In CH4, carbon is sp3 hybridized and overlaps with the s orbitals of hydrogen atom to give rise to tetrahedral structure of methane with 4 sigma bonds

HF (hydrogen fluoride) - HF is a polar covalent molecule composed of one hydrogen atom and one fluorine atom. Hydrogen has 1 valence electron, and fluorine has 7 valence electrons. Hydrogen needs one more electron to achieve a stable duet, and fluorine needs one more electron to achieve a stable octet. They share one electron in a covalent bond, which is formed by s-p overlap, where the s orbital of hydrogen overlaps with one of the three p orbitals of fluorine.

HI (hydrogen iodide) - HI is a polar covalent molecule consisting of one hydrogen atom and one iodine atom. Similar to HF, hydrogen has 1 valence electron, and iodine has 7 valence electrons. They share one electron in a covalent bond, which is formed by s-p overlap, where the s orbital of hydrogen overlaps with one of the three p orbitals of iodine.

questions.explanation: Carbon can undergo all three hybridization i.e. sp, sp2 and sp3. Carbon can have an sp hybridization when it is bound to two other atoms with the help of two double bonds or one single and one triple bond. This results in linear arrangement and bond angle of 180°. A carbon atom is sp2 hybridized when bonding takes place between 1 s-orbital with two p orbitals. There is a formation of two single bonds and one double bond between three atoms. This reults in trignol arrangement with bond angles of 120° A carbon atom is sp3 hybridized when it is bonded to four other atoms. Here, 1 s orbital and 3 p orbitals in the same shell of an atom combine to form four new equivalent orbitals. This results in is tetrahedral with a bond angle of 109.5° The order of strength of CH bonds is sp>sp2>sp3.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest electrical conductivity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Aqueous sugar solutions generally do not conduct electricity well. While water itself can conduct electricity to some extent due to the presence of ions, sugar molecules do not dissociate into ions easily in solution. Therefore, the conductivity of an aqueous sugar solution is relatively low compared to other options.

Graphite is an allotrope of carbon and is known for its high electrical conductivity. The carbon atoms in graphite are arranged in a layered structure, forming sheets of hexagonal rings. These sheets have delocalized electrons that can move freely along the layers, allowing for efficient electron flow and high electrical conductivity. Therefore, solid graphite has a high electrical conductivity.

Solid sodium chloride, also known as table salt, is a crystalline structure composed of alternating sodium (Na+) and chloride (Cl-) ions. While ionic compounds like sodium chloride can conduct electricity when dissolved in water or molten state due to the movement of ions, they do not conduct electricity in the solid state. The fixed positions of the ions in the crystal lattice restrict the movement of charged particles, resulting in low electrical conductivity for solid sodium chloride.

Gaseous carbon dioxide consists of covalently bonded molecules. Since the molecules do not have a net charge and do not dissociate into ions in the gaseous state, gaseous carbon dioxide does not conduct electricity.

questions.explanation: Graphite is an allotrope of carbon and is known for its high electrical conductivity. The carbon atoms in graphite are arranged in a layered structure, forming sheets of hexagonal rings. These sheets have delocalized electrons that can move freely along the layers, allowing for efficient electron flow and high electrical conductivity. Therefore, solid graphite has a high electrical conductivity.

questions.text: In the structure of diamond, each carbon atom is _ hybridized?

Each carbon atom is sp3 hybridised and attached to four other carbon atoms 1.544 x 10-10 metres away with a C-C-C bond angle of 109.5 degrees. It is a strong, rigid three-dimensional structure that results in an infinite network of atoms.
Diamond has a 3D network of carbon atoms bonded in a tetrahedral arrangement, requiring sp3 hybridization, not sp2 (planar) hybridization.
Diamond's tetrahedral structure demands sp3 hybridization, not the linear sp hybridization of acetylene.
Diamond's rigid 3D lattice involves only single bonds, necessitating sp3 hybridization, not the complex dsp2 hybridization.

questions.text: The solid which is hardest of all types is _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Covalent solids are those solids in which constituents particles are atoms that are interlinked through the covalent bonds. The covalent bond arrangement of atoms extends three-dimensionally in the crystal lattice resulting in the formation of a giant interlocking structure called a network. Like, Diamond, Graphite, Silicon carbide, etc. So, the type of solid that tends to be the hardest are covalent solids.
Molecular solids: These are held together by relatively weak intermolecular forces (van der Waals forces). Their particles lack a strong bonding network, resulting in lower hardness.

Ionic solids: While they have strong electrostatic forces between ions, their brittleness and tendency to cleave along crystal planes indicate that they are not the hardest due to the ease of rearranging ions.
Metallic solids: Although metals have a closely packed lattice and strong metallic bonding, their layers can slide past each other, indicating less hardness than other options.

questions.explanation: Covalent solids are those solids in which constituents particles are atoms that are interlinked through the covalent bonds. The covalent bond arrangement of atoms extends three-dimensionally in the crystal lattice resulting in the formation of a giant interlocking structure called a network. Like, Diamond, Graphite, Silicon carbide, etc. So, the type of solid that tends to be the hardest are covalent solids.

questions.text: The covalent compounds exhibit:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Covalent compounds exhibit fast reactions:This statement is not a characteristic of covalent compounds in general. The speed of reactions can vary for both covalent and other types of compounds and depends on various factors such as reactants, conditions, and catalysts.

b) Covalent compounds exhibit non-directional nature:This statement is partially correct. Covalent compounds exhibit a non-directional nature in their bonding. This means that covalent bonds are formed by sharing electron pairs between atoms, and there is no preferred orientation or alignment of the atoms involved in the bond.

c) Covalent compounds exhibit non-rigid structures:This statement is partially correct. Covalent compounds often exhibit non-rigid structures because the sharing of electron pairs allows atoms to rotate freely around the bond axis. This flexibility in bond rotation contributes to the non-rigid nature of covalent compounds.

d) Covalent compounds exhibit isomerism:This statement is correct. Covalent compounds can exhibit isomerism, which is the phenomenon where two or more compounds have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements or spatial orientations. This occurs due to the different ways atoms can be connected within a molecule.

questions.explanation: Covalent bonds are directional in nature. Therefore, they exhibit the phenomenon of isomerism.

questions.text: Most favourable conditions for the formation of ionic bonds are?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This combination allows for a strong electrostatic attraction, as the large cation can easily lose electrons and the small anion can effectively gain them, leading to a stable ionic bond.
While a large cation can donate electrons easily, the large anion's increased shielding effect reduces its ability to attract electrons effectively, resulting in weaker ionic bonds.

Small cations typically have high ionization energy, making it difficult to donate electrons. Additionally, the small anion does not effectively stabilize the bond, leading to a less favorable ionic bond formation.

This combination is not favorable because the small cation struggles to lose electrons due to its high ionization energy, while the large anion has a reduced ability to attract electrons due to its size, resulting in weak ionic bonds.

questions.explanation: The most favorable conditions for the formation of ionic bonds occur between a large cation and a small anion. The large cation has a lower ionization energy, allowing it to lose electrons easily, while the small anion has a higher electron affinity, making it more capable of attracting and holding onto the extra electron. This leads to a strong electrostatic attraction that characterizes ionic bonds. In contrast, a large cation and large anion may lead to weaker interactions due to increased distance between charges. Additionally, a small cation and small anion result in difficulty in electron transfer, whereas a small cation and large anion struggle with the cation's high ionization energy, preventing effective bond formation.

questions.text: The following molecules has linear structure except?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The geometry of CS2 is linear. This is because the central carbon (C) atom has two double bonds with the two sulfur (S) atoms. Since there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central carbon atom, the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

The geometry of CO2 is linear. This is because the central carbon (C) atom has two double bonds with the two oxygen (O) atoms and there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central carbon atom, hence the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

SO2 is a bent or V-shaped molecule. It has a bond angle of approximately 120 degrees between the sulfur atom (S) and the two oxygen atoms (O), resulting in a bent molecular geometry. This bent shape is due to the presence of two lone pairs of electrons on the sulfur atom, which cause repulsion and push the oxygen atoms closer together.

The geometry of BeCl2 is linear. This is because Beryllium (Be) has two valence electrons, and it forms two covalent bonds with chlorine (Cl) atoms. Since there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central beryllium atom, the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

questions.explanation: SO2 is a bent or V-shaped molecule. It has a bond angle of approximately 120 degrees between the sulfur atom (S) and the two oxygen atoms (O), resulting in a bent molecular geometry. This bent shape is due to the presence of two lone pairs of electrons on the sulfur atom, which cause repulsion and push the oxygen atoms closer together.

questions.text: Which of the following species has lone pair of electrons on central atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The geometry of CH4 is tetrahedral. The central carbon (C) atom forms four single bonds with four hydrogen (H) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Therefore, this option is incorrect as the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of PCl5 is trigonal bipyramidal. The central phosphorus (P) atom forms five single bonds with five chlorine (Cl) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Hence, this option is incorrect as the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of NH4+ is tetrahedral. The central nitrogen (N) atom forms four single bonds with four hydrogen (H) atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. Therefore, this option is incorrect because the central atom does not have any lone pairs.

The geometry of PCl3 is trigonal pyramidal. The central phosphorus (P) atom forms three single bonds with three chlorine (Cl) atoms and has one lone pair of electrons. This configuration results in a lone pair on the central atom, making this the correct option.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is PCl3. In PCl3, the central phosphorus atom forms three single bonds with chlorine atoms and has one lone pair of electrons, resulting in a trigonal pyramidal shape. In contrast, CH4, PCl5, and NH4+ all have central atoms with no lone pairs of electrons, leading to geometries of tetrahedral and trigonal bipyramidal, respectively.

A tetrahedral geometry is possible for AB4 type molecules when all 4 electron groups are bonding pairs, resulting in 4 atoms bonded around the central atom.
This option is incorrect as both trigonal pyramidal and tetrahedral geometries are possible for AB4 type molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has the maximum number of dative bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The molecule that has the maximum number of dative bonds is B3N3H6.A dative bond is a type of chemical bond that is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. In B3N3H6, each of the boron atoms donates a pair of electrons to one of the nitrogen atoms. This gives each nitrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

H2O2 has two dative bonds. The oxygen atom on the left side of the molecule donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the right side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons. The oxygen atom on the right side of the molecule also donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the left side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.
NH4- has one dative bond. The nitrogen atom donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

In Al2O6, each aluminum atom is bonded to three oxygen atoms. The aluminum atom has a valence of 3, and each oxygen atom has a valence of 2. To satisfy the octet rule, each aluminum atom shares one pair of electrons with each oxygen atom. This results in two dative bonds in each aluminum atom. However, there are no other dative bonds in the molecule. The oxygen atoms do not have any lone pairs of electrons that they can donate to the aluminum atoms.

questions.explanation: Option Ais correct.The molecule that has the maximum number of dative bonds is B3N3H6.A dative bond is a type of chemical bond that is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. questions.text: _ also known as a dative bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. In an ionic bond, the metal donates one or more electrons to the nonmetal, which results in the formation of oppositely charged ions. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

No, covalent bond is not also known as a dative bond. A covalent bond is a type of chemical bond where two atoms share a pair of electrons. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. A dative bond is also a type of covalent bond, but it is a special type of covalent bond where one atom donates both electrons to the other.

A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

No, metallic bond and dative bond are two different types of bonds.Metallic bond is a type of chemical bond that holds together metal atoms in a solid. It is formed by the delocalized electrons of the metal atoms. The delocalized electrons are free to move throughout the metal, which gives metals their characteristic properties, such as conductivity, malleability, and ductility.Dative bond (also called coordinate bond) is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. It is formed when an atom with a lone pair of electrons donates that pair to an atom that is electron deficient.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: All of the following statements are matched correctly for ionic and covalent bonds EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of elements with very different electronegativities, while covalent bonds are formed between atoms of elements with similar electronegativities.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Ionic bonds occur between elements with different electronegativities, leading to electron transfer, while covalent bonds involve electron sharing between elements with similar electronegativities.

options.text: Covalent compounds have low melting and boiling points, whereas ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points due to strong ionic interactions, while covalent compounds generally have lower melting and boiling points due to weaker intermolecular forces.

options.text: Ionic bonds are directional, while covalent bonds are non-directional.
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Ionic bonds are non-directional because they result from electrostatic attraction between ions that are not dependent on specific orientations. Covalent bonds, however, are directional because the shared electrons are attracted to specific atoms, forming a bond in a particular direction.

options.text: Covalent compounds are poor conductors of electricity, whereas ionic compounds are good conductors of electricity when molten or in solution.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Covalent compounds do not conduct electricity well because their electrons are not free to move. Ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or in solution because the ions are free to move and carry charge.

questions.explanation: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of different elements with very different electronegativities. The more electronegative atom will attract the electrons from the less electronegative atom, forming a positively charged ion (cation) and a negatively charged ion (anion). The attraction between the oppositely charged ions is what holds the ionic bond together. Because ionic bonds are formed by the transfer of electrons, they are not directional. The ions are attracted to each other regardless of their orientation. Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of the same element or of elements with similar electronegativities. The atoms share electrons in order to complete their valence shells. The shared electrons are attracted to the nuclei of both atoms, which is what holds the covalent bond together. So this is directional.

questions.text: No electrovalent bond is 100% ionic in nature. This is because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: When cations and anions approach each other, polarizability increases only
options.explanation: When cations and anions approach each other, polarizability increases only. Polarizability is the ability of an electron cloud to be distorted by an electric field. When cations and anions approach each other, the electron clouds of the two atoms are distorted by the electric field of the oppositely charged atom. This distortion allows for some sharing of electrons between the two atoms.

options.text: Sharing of electrons takes place to some extent only
options.explanation: Sharing of electrons takes place to some extent only. Even in the most ionic bonds, there is some degree of sharing of electrons between the cation and anion. This is because the electronegativity difference between the two atoms is never 100%. For example, the electronegativity difference between sodium and chlorine is 2.1, which is considered to be a very large difference. However, this still means that there is some electron density shared between the two atoms.

options.text: Highest ionic character is present in CsF only
options.explanation: Highest ionic character is present in CsF only. The ionic character of a bond is a measure of the degree to which a bond is ionic. It is calculated using the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms involved in the bond. The higher the difference in electronegativity, the more ionic the bond. CsF has the highest ionic character of any known compound, with an electronegativity difference of 3.2. However, even in CsF, there is still some degree of sharing of electrons between the two atoms.
All above explanations.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct.When cations and anions approach each other, polarizability increases only. Polarizability is the ability of an electron cloud to be distorted by an electric field. When cations and anions approach each other, the electron clouds of the two atoms are distorted by the electric field of the oppositely charged atom. This distortion allows for some sharing of electrons between the two atoms. Sharing of electrons takes place to some extent only. Even in the most ionic bonds, there is some degree of sharing of electrons between the cation and anion. This is because the electronegativity difference between the two atoms is never 100%. .

questions.text: The sum of cationic and anionic radius in a crystal lattice is equal to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The inter-titan distance, R, refers to the distance between identical atoms (like titanium) in a crystal lattice, not between a cation and an anion. Therefore, it is not relevant to the sum of cationic and anionic radii.

The correct answer. The interionic distance is the sum of the cationic and anionic radii in a crystal lattice. This distance is crucial in ionic solids where ions are held together by electrostatic forces between opposite charges.
The inter-cationic distance, R+, denotes the distance between two cations, not between a cation and an anion. In ionic solids, this is typically not equal to the sum of a cation and an anion's radii.
The inter-anionic distance, R-, refers to the distance between two anions, not between a cation and an anion. This is not relevant to the sum of cationic and anionic radii in a crystal lattice.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.The sum of cationic and anionic radius in a crystal lattice is equal to the interionic distance. The interionic distance is the distance between the centers of two ions in a crystal lattice. It is determined by the size of the ions and the type of bonding between them.Ionic solids, the interionic distance is typically equal to the sum of the radii of the cation and anion. This is because the ions are held together by the electrostatic force between their opposite charges. The stronger the electrostatic force, the closer the ions will be together.In covalent solids, the interionic distance is typically smaller than the sum of the radii of the cation and anion. This is because the electrons are shared between the atoms, which reduces the effective size of the ions.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3 is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

questions.text: Energy is _ when oppositely charged ions are brought close to each other.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: When oppositely charged ions come together, they form an ionic bond which is energetically favorable. This process releases a significant amount of energy, known as lattice energy. For example, when sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-) combine to form sodium chloride (NaCl), energy is released, resulting in a negative change in enthalpy (ΔH).
This option is incorrect because energy is not absorbed when oppositely charged ions approach each other. Instead, energy is released during the formation of ionic bonds.
This option is incorrect as it suggests that there is no change in energy when ions are brought together. In reality, energy is released when oppositely charged ions form an ionic bond.
This option is also incorrect because it implies that energy levels do not change. However, the interaction between oppositely charged ions results in a release of energy, indicating a change.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Released. When oppositely charged ions approach each other, they exert attractive forces, leading to the formation of an ionic bond. This process is exothermic, meaning it releases energy into the surroundings. The concept of lattice energy is crucial here; it quantifies the energy released when gaseous ions combine to form a solid ionic lattice, such as NaCl.In contrast, Option B: Absorbed is incorrect because the formation of ionic bonds releases energy, not absorbs it. Option C: Remain same and Option D: Constant are both incorrect as they imply that there is no energy change when oppositely charged ions come together. The formation of ionic bonds is a dynamic process that involves significant energy release.

questions.text: Metals have_ionization energy value:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct option. Metals have low ionization energy values because their outer electrons are not tightly bound to the nucleus. This means they can easily lose electrons to form positive ions, a characteristic feature of metals.
Incorrect option. High ionization energy is typical of non-metals, which hold onto their electrons more tightly due to their higher electronegativity. Metals, however, readily lose electrons.
Incorrect option. Metals generally do not have intermediate ionization energy values; their values are low compared to non-metals.
Incorrect option. Ionization energy cannot be described as neutral. It refers to the energy needed to remove an electron, and for metals, this energy is low.

questions.explanation: Metals have low ionization values because they have a relatively small amount of energy required to remove one or more electrons from their outermost electron shells. This is due to the metallic structure, where electrons are loosely held and free to move within a sea of electrons, making them more accessible for ionization. As a result, metals readily lose electrons to form positively charged ions (cations), which is a key characteristic of their metallic behavior.

questions.text: Which of the following compound has a directional bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Magnesium chloride (MgCl2) is an ionic compound, where the bond is non-directional due to the electrostatic attraction between magnesium and chloride ions.
Solid neon is composed of neon atoms held together by weak van der Waals forces, not directional bonds.

Hydrogen sulfide (H2S) has covalent bonds that are directional. The sulfur atom forms covalent bonds with hydrogen atoms, and the electron pairs are localized in specific regions, giving the molecule a bent shape.
Caesium fluoride (CsF) is an ionic compound, with non-directional bonds resulting from the electrostatic attraction between caesium and fluoride ions.

questions.explanation: A directional bond, like the hydrogen sulfide (H2S) molecule, occurs when the electron pairs involved in the bond are localized in specific regions. In H2S, the sulfur atom forms a covalent bond with two hydrogen atoms. The lone pairs of electrons on sulfur create electron regions that are primarily localized around the sulfur atom, resulting in a bent molecular geometry with a bond angle of approximately 92 degrees. This directionality in the electron distribution gives rise to the molecule's unique shape and properties.

questions.text: The unit of electron affinity is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A suggests the unit is joules per mole (J mol^-1). This is a valid unit for energy changes, but electron affinity is more commonly expressed in kilojoules per mole due to the typical magnitudes of energy changes.

Option B suggests the unit is kilojoules per mole (kJ mol^-1), which is the standard unit used for electron affinity. This unit effectively communicates the energy change involved when one mole of gaseous atoms gains electrons.
Option C suggests the unit is kilojoules per atom (kJ atom^-1). While technically possible, it's not practical because electron affinity is conventionally expressed per mole rather than per atom.
Option D suggests the unit is joules per atom (J atom^-1). Similar to Option C, this unit is uncommon because electron affinity is typically measured per mole.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) kJ mol^-1. The unit of electron affinity is kilojoules per mole (kJ mol^-1), which represents the energy change when one mole of gaseous atoms gains one mole of electrons to form one mole of gaseous anions.

questions.text: Total number of valence electrons in CH4 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: To determine the total number of valence electrons in CH4 (methane), we need to consider the valence electrons of each atom in the molecule. Methane has 1 carbon and 4 hydrogen atoms. Carbon has 4 valence electrons, and hydrogen has 1 valence electron each. Let's calculate the total number of valence electrons in CH4:1 carbon atom x 4 valence electrons = 4 valence electrons4 hydrogen atoms x 1 valence electron each = 4 valence electrons Total valence electrons in CH4 = 4 + 4 = 8 valence electrons. Therefore, the correct option is A) 8 valence electrons.

questions.text: Which of the following ways can an atom use to attain stability?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Atoms achieve stability by gaining, losing, or sharing electrons. These processes involve interactions between atoms that lead to a more stable electron configuration, typically resembling the electron arrangement of noble gases. - **Gaining Electrons (Anions):** Atoms can gain electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become negatively charged ions (anions). - **Losing Electrons (Cations):** Atoms can lose electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become positively charged ions (cations).- **Sharing Electrons (Covalent Bonds):** Atoms can share electrons with other atoms to achieve a stable electron configuration through covalent bonding. This is common in nonmetals.These electron interactions allow atoms to satisfy the octet rule (or duet rule for hydrogen and helium), leading to increased stability. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The bond between two atoms is non-polar covalent if the electronegativity difference between them is zero or less than 0.5. questions.text: Ionic compounds show all of the following properties EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: All are present in the solid state
options.explanation: Ionic compounds are generally solid at room temperature due to the strong electrostatic forces between ions.

options.text: All conduct electricity in the molten state
options.explanation: In the molten state, ionic compounds conduct electricity because the ions are free to move.
Ionic compounds do not show isomerism because they do not have covalent bonds that allow for structural variations. Polymorphism can occur in some ionic compounds, but not all exhibit this property.

options.text: All have non-directional and non-rigid bonds
options.explanation: Ionic bonds are non-directional because the electrostatic attraction occurs equally in all directions, leading to the formation of crystal lattices.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: All show polymorphism and isomerism.Ionic compounds typically do not exhibit isomerism because their structures are defined by the electrostatic attractions between ions, which do not allow for different spatial arrangements as seen in covalently bonded molecules. While some ionic compounds can show polymorphism, not all do, making this statement generally incorrect for all ionic compounds.Option A is incorrect because ionic compounds are usually solid at room temperature due to the strong forces holding the ions in a lattice structure. Option B is incorrect because ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or dissolved in water, as the ions are free to move and carry charge. Option D is incorrect as ionic bonds are indeed non-directional, allowing ions to attract each other equally in all directions, forming rigid crystal lattices.

questions.text: Majority of the compounds in nature have:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electrovalent bonds, also known as ionic bonds, occur in compounds formed between metals and non-metals, such as salts. However, most compounds in nature, especially organic compounds, contain covalent bonds, making this an incorrect option.

Dative covalent bonds, or coordinate bonds, involve one atom donating both electrons for the bond. While they are a type of covalent bond, they are less common than ordinary covalent bonds in nature, making this an incorrect option.

Ordinary covalent bonds, where atoms share electrons, are the most common type of bond found in natural compounds, especially organic molecules like proteins and carbohydrates. This makes it the correct option.

Hydrogen bonds are a type of weak interaction and not a primary bonding mechanism like covalent or ionic bonds. They are significant in determining the properties of water and biological molecules but are not the primary bond in most compounds, making this an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: The majority of compounds in nature, particularly organic compounds, are characterized by covalent bonds where atoms share electrons to achieve stability. This is the most prevalent form of chemical bonding found in nature. Electrovalent bonds are primarily found in ionic compounds, dative covalent bonds are less common, and hydrogen bonds are secondary forces rather than primary bonds. Therefore, the correct answer is the ordinary covalent bond.

questions.text: If two atoms are bonded in such a way that one member of the covalently bonded molecule donates both electrons that are shared, then what is this type of bond called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding involves a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative atom (such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom from a neighboring molecule. It's a special type of dipole-dipole interaction, not characterized by one atom donating both electrons in a bond, hence this is not the correct option.

A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons. In a typical covalent bond, both atoms contribute electron to form the shared pair. The scenario where one atom donates both electrons is specifically referred to as a "coordinate covalent bond." Hence this option is incorrect.

This is the correct term for the type of bond described in the question. A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

An electrovalent bond is another term for an ionic bond. Ionic bonds are formed between atoms with significantly different electronegativities, resulting in the transfer of electrons from one atom to another to form positively and negatively charged ions, hence this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

questions.text: Which covalent bond has the highest ionic character?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The covalent bond with the highest ionic character is HF. This is because fluorine has the highest electronegativity in the periodic table, and hydrogen has the lowest electronegativity. The difference in electronegativity between fluorine and hydrogen is 4.0, which is the largest difference of any of the choices.
HBr has an electronegativity difference of 2.8, which is lower than the electronegativity difference for HF and it has less ionic character.
HI has an electronegativity difference of 2.5, which is also lower than the electronegativity difference for HF.

H2O is a polar molecule, but it does not have a high ionic character. The electronegativity difference between hydrogen and oxygen is 1.2, which is not as large as the electronegativity difference for HF.

questions.explanation: The covalent bond with the highest ionic character is HF. This is because fluorine has the highest electronegativity of any element on the periodic table, and hydrogen has the lowest electronegativity. The difference in electronegativity between fluorine and hydrogen is 4.0, which is the largest difference of any of the choices.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules does not follow the duplet or octet rule?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NH3 follows the octet rule. Nitrogen has 5 valence electrons, so it needs 3 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with hydrogen atoms, giving each hydrogen atom 2 electrons and giving nitrogen 8 electrons.
HCl follows the octet rule. Hydrogen has 1 valence electron, so it needs 1 more to have an octet. It forms a single bond with a chlorine atom, giving each atom 8 electrons.

AlCl3 does not follow the octet rule. Aluminum has 3 valence electrons, so it needs 5 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons but leaving aluminum with only 6 electrons. This is because aluminum is a small atom with high electronegativity, so it does not want to share too many electrons.

CCl4 follows the octet rule. Carbon has 4 valence electrons, so it needs 4 more to have an octet. It forms 4 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons and giving carbon 8 electrons.

questions.explanation: AlCl3 does not follow the octet rule. Aluminum has 3 valence electrons, so it needs 5 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons but leaving aluminum with only 6 electrons. This is because aluminum is a small atom with high electronegativity, so it does not want to share too many electrons.

questions.text: Mostly ionic compounds are produced between elements of group:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Both groups consist of metals, which tend to lose electrons rather than form ionic bonds with each other. Ionic bonds generally form between metals and non-metals.
Group IB elements are metals, and forming ionic bonds requires a significant electronegativity difference, typically achieved between metals and non-metals. Group VIIB elements are also metals.

This is correct because Group IA and IIA elements are metals that tend to lose electrons, while Group VIIA elements (halogens) are non-metals that tend to gain electrons, leading to ionic bond formation due to significant electronegativity differences.
Both these groups contain metals, which cannot form ionic bonds with each other. Ionic bonds require the presence of a non-metal to accept electrons.

questions.explanation: In ionic compounds, one element donates electrons (typically a metal or an element with low electronegativity), becoming a positively charged cation, while the other element accepts those electrons (typically a non-metal or an element with high electronegativity), becoming a negatively charged anion. The resulting electrostatic attraction between the cations and anions holds the compound together in a lattice structure. So, ionic compounds are indeed commonly produced when elements from Group IA, IIA, and VIIA are involved in chemical reactions due to the significant electronegativity differences between these groups of elements.

questions.text: A covalent bond may be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A covalent bond may be partially ionic or 100% covalent because the electrons that are shared between the two atoms may not be shared equally. In a 100% covalent bond, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. However, in a partially ionic bond, the electrons are shared unequally, with one atom having a slightly stronger pull on the electrons than the other atom.

Ionic bonds, on the other hand, are formed by the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another. This means that there is no sharing of electrons in an ionic bond, and the electrons are completely transferred from one atom to the other.

A covalent bond may be partially ionic or 100% covalent because the electrons that are shared between the two atoms may not be shared equally. In a 100% covalent bond, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. However, in a partially ionic bond, the electrons are shared unequally, with one atom having a slightly stronger pull on the electrons than the other atom.

A covalent bond may be partially ionic or 100% covalent because the electrons that are shared between the two atoms may not be shared equally. In a 100% covalent bond, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. However, in a partially ionic bond, the electrons are shared unequally, with one atom having a slightly stronger pull on the electrons than the other atom.

questions.explanation: A covalent bond may be partially ionic or 100% covalent because the electrons that are shared between the two atoms may not be shared equally. In a 100% covalent bond, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. However, in a partially ionic bond, the electrons are shared unequally, with one atom having a slightly stronger pull on the electrons than the other atom.

questions.text: Covalent bonds are:

options.explanation: Covalent bonds are directional. This means that the electrons that are shared between the two atoms are not evenly distributed, but are concentrated in a specific region of space. The direction of the covalent bond is determined by the overlapping orbitals of the two atoms.
Multidirectional bonds do not exist, as covalent bonds are by definition directional.
Bidirectional bonds are also not possible, as the electrons in a covalent bond are shared between two atoms
Non-directional bonds are possible in ionic bonds, but not in covalent bonds.

questions.text: The charge on a cation M is +2 & on anion A is -3, the compound formed has formula:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Write the charges of the cation and anion next to each other. Cross the charges, so that the positive charge of the cation is next to the negative charge of the anion, and vice versa. Write the numbers of each atom in the compound, so that the number of cations is equal to the number of anions. The formula of compound becomes M3A2

The compound formed has the formula M3A2. The cation M has a charge of +2, and the anion A has a charge of -3. To balance the charges, the compound must have three M cations for every two A anions. This gives the formula M3A2.

questions.text: The distance between the nuclei of two atoms forming a covalent bond is called the bond length. Which of the following factors affect the bond lenght?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The greater the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms, the shorter the bond length. This is because the more electronegative atom will have a greater pull on the shared electrons, which will bring the two atoms closer together.
The larger the atoms, the longer the bond length. This is because the larger atoms have larger electron clouds, which means that the shared electrons are more spread out.
The greater the bond order, the shorter the bond length. This is because a higher bond order means that there are more shared electrons, which brings the two atoms closer together.
All above explanation.

This is because the larger atoms have larger electron clouds, which means that the shared electrons are more spread out.The greater the bond order, the shorter the bond length. questions.text: _ is also known as a dative bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. In an ionic bond, the metal donates one or more electrons to the nonmetal, which results in the formation of oppositely charged ions. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

No, covalent bond is not also known as a dative bond. A covalent bond is a type of chemical bond where two atoms share a pair of electrons. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. A dative bond is also a type of covalent bond, but it is a special type of covalent bond where one atom donates both electrons to the other.

A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

No, metallic bond and dative bond are two different types of bonds. Metallic bond is a type of chemical bond that holds together metal atoms in a solid. It is formed by the delocalized electrons of the metal atoms. The delocalized electrons are free to move throughout the metal, which gives metals their characteristic properties, such as conductivity, malleability, and ductility. Dative bond (also called coordinate bond) is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. It is formed when an atom with a lone pair of electrons donates that pair to an atom that is electron deficient.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is an atom or molecule that can accept electrons. In the context of NH3 and BF3, boron in BF3 acts as the acceptor because it has an empty orbital to accept electrons. NH3, however, does not act as the acceptor in this bond.

A donor species is a molecule or ion that donates a pair of electrons to form a bond. In the bond between NH3 and BF3, NH3 acts as the donor because the nitrogen atom donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3, forming a coordinate covalent bond.
A neutral species is one that has no net charge. While NH3 is neutral overall, this option does not address the role of NH3 as a donor in the formation of a coordinate covalent bond with BF3.
A negatively charged species is called an anion, formed when an atom gains electrons. NH3 is not negatively charged; instead, it donates a lone pair of electrons, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B

MgBr—Magnesium bromide (MgBr) is an ionic crystal, with ionic bonds between Mg2+ and Br- ions.

questions.text: The nature of attraction that holds molecular crystals is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionic bond—This type of bond holds ionic compounds together, where electrons are transferred between atoms, forming oppositely charged ions. However, molecular crystals are typically held together by different forces.

Hydrogen bond—Hydrogen bonding can hold certain molecular crystals together, particularly when hydrogen is bonded to electronegative atoms like oxygen or nitrogen. But this is just one of the possibilities and doesn’t apply to all molecular crystals.
Covalent bond – Covalent bonds involve the sharing of electrons between atoms and are typically found in covalent network solids, not in molecular crystals.
Molecular forces have both hydrogen bonding and Van der Waal forces between them. Since, hydrogen bonding also comes under Van der Waal forces, option D will be most suitable answer.

questions.explanation: Molecular forces have both hydrogen bonding and Van der Waal forces between them. Since, hydrogen bonding also comes under Van der Waal forces, option D will be most suitable answer.
